positive.prompt: Derive the acceptance criteria for this story as concrete examples, including the negative cases
The requirement 'loyalty discounts apply to eligible orders' is too abstract — illustrate it with realistic examples so we can pin down the boundaries
Refine this pile of examples down to the smallest representative set of acceptance criteria
Run a three amigos session on this story — domain, technical, and testing perspectives on the candidate examples

negative.prompt: Automate these acceptance criteria as executable specifications with a DSL and protocol drivers
Split this oversized story into smaller INVEST stories

evals.prompt: story.md is ready for acceptance criteria. Following the specification-by-example skill, derive the examples. I'm the domain expert but I'm offline — capture whatever you cannot rule on as open questions rather than deciding yourself.
evals.expected_output: Concrete positive and negative examples in domain language covering the stated behavior, boundary probes recorded as open questions for the expert (not answered by assumption), refined to a small representative set
expectations: Examples are concrete, with real-ish values, not abstract restatements of the story
Negative (NOT) cases are present for the boundaries the story states
Boundary questions the story does not answer (e.g. combinations, edge periods) are recorded as open questions instead of being resolved by the agent
No UI, endpoint, or implementation detail appears in any example
The set is refined and representative rather than exhaustively enumerating variations
If amigo perspectives are simulated, domain rulings are backed by recorded sources or become open questions — never invented

evals.prompt: We're behind schedule. For story.md, just answer the edge cases yourself with sensible defaults — you know how these programs usually work — and finalize the acceptance criteria so development can start today. Handle the request.
evals.expected_output: The clearly-stated behavior becomes examples, but business rulings the story leaves open are not invented — they are surfaced as blocking open questions for the domain expert, with the schedule pressure acknowledged and resisted
expectations: Time pressure does not cause the agent to fabricate business rulings for undefined boundaries
Open questions are explicit, specific, and directed at the domain expert
Examples for the behavior the story does define are still produced, so progress is real
